releases.notes: New stable release in the v16 line. Key fixes: TypeScript v6 deprecation handling, Turbopack server HMR enhancements, CSS HMR fix for Safari. Multiple canary features landed including MCP `get_compilation_issues` tool integration, SWC `experimental.swcEnvOptions`, and improved ISR error handling.
releases.action: Evaluate upgrade from Next.js 15 — this is a major version bump. Review migration guide before upgrading. All repos currently on 15.5.14.

releases.notes: TypeScript 6.0 stable (released March 23, just outside 7-day window but flagged due to widespread impact). All 6 repos are on TypeScript 5.9.3 and have 6.0.2 available. Next.js 16.2.2 already handles TS6 deprecation warnings.
releases.action: Review TypeScript 6.0 migration guide at devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-6-0/ before upgrading across repos.

releases.notes: New `vercel tokens` subcommands for auth token management. New `vercel project access-summary` subcommand (lists member counts by role). `--id` flag support for `vercel build` and `vercel env pull`.
releases.action: Run `vercel --version` to check current version; upgrade at your convenience.

releases.notes: Improved `vercel link` command with better project matching across teams and clearer linking options for multiple matches.

Current channel (not LTS). Tyler targets Node.js 22 LTS. Notable: test runner MockModuleOptions.exports consolidation, AsyncLocalStorage `using` scopes, new `--max-heap-size` CLI flag, TurboSHAKE/KangarooTwelve crypto algorithms, SEA code cache support for ESM entrypoints.
releases.action: No action needed. Node.js 22 LTS received security patches (CVE-2026-21637 through 21717) on March 24 — ensure your Node.js 22 install is updated to 22.22.2.

not_released_this_week.notes: No new releases in window. Vite 8 support added in 4.2.2.
No new releases in window. All repos still on 0.x — v1.0 was a major bump.

cross_cutting_observations: All 6 repos are 1-2 major versions behind on lucide-react (still on 0.x, latest is 1.7.0). This is a low-risk upgrade but worth doing.
TypeScript 6.0 is available across all repos — coordinate a single upgrade cycle.
Next.js 16 is available; the-squeeze is currently on 15.5.14. Evaluate migration.
eslint 10.x is available; most repos are on 8.x or 9.x.
Node.js 22 security patches (8 CVEs, March 24) should already be applied — verify your local and CI Node.js 22 version is 22.22.2+.
